Rule: Calculate button is enabled when all ASW fields are inputted
Test Case: TC-01 - All mandatory fields inputted enables Calculate
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the ASW section.
Action: Enter valid values in all mandatory ASW fields
Expected Result: All mandatory ASW fields contain valid values
Calculate button is enabled
Message is shown: All fields in Axle Spacing and Weights are required to calculate results.

Rule: Calculate button is enabled when all ASW fields are inputted
Test Case: TC-02 - Missing mandatory data disables Calculate
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the ASW section.
Action: Enter values in only some mandatory ASW fields
Expected Result: At least one mandatory ASW field remains empty or invalid
Calculate button is not enabled
Message is shown: All fields in Axle Spacing and Weights are required to calculate results.

Rule: Users must input all ASW fields before calculation is possible either manually or when attempting to continue to review and confirm
Test Case: TC-03 - New vehicle requires ASW completion again
Preconditions: User has already completed a valid ASW calculation.
Action: Add a new vehicle to the application
Expected Result: ASW table updates to include axle units for the new vehicle

Action: Review ASW controls after table update
Expected Result: Calculate button is not enabled until new mandatory ASW fields are completed

Rule: Users may optionally initiate manual calculation of the ASW
Test Case: TC-04 - Manual calculation with partial input
Preconditions: User is logged in and on the ASW section with partial mandatory data entered.
Expected Result: Calculation is not executed
Message is shown: All fields in Axle Spacing and Weights are required to calculate results.

Action: Attempt to continue to next stage
Expected Result: User cannot continue

Rule: Users may optionally initiate manual calculation of the ASW
Test Case: TC-05 - Manual calculation with all inputted
Preconditions: User is logged in and all mandatory ASW fields contain valid values.
Expected Result: Calculation is executed
Calculation results are displayed

Action: Attempt to continue to next stage
Expected Result: User can continue

Rule: The ASW is automatically calculated when a user attempts to continue to review and confirm if all ASW fields are inputted
Test Case: TC-06 - Continue with partial input blocks progression
Preconditions: User is logged in with partial mandatory ASW data entered.
Action: Click Continue to Review and Confirm
Expected Result: Automatic calculation cannot complete
Message is shown: All fields in Axle Spacing and Weights are required to calculate results.
User remains on application form and cannot continue

Rule: The ASW is automatically calculated when a user attempts to continue to review and confirm if all ASW fields are inputted
Test Case: TC-07 - Continue with all inputted and no errors
Preconditions: User is logged in and all mandatory ASW fields are valid with no calculation errors.
Action: Click Continue to Review and Confirm
Expected Result: Automatic ASW calculation runs
Calculation results are shown
User is directed to the Review and Confirm Details page

Rule: The ASW is automatically calculated when a user attempts to continue to review and confirm if all ASW fields are inputted
Test Case: TC-08 - Continue with all inputted and errors
Preconditions: User is logged in and all mandatory ASW fields are valid but produce bridge calculation errors.
Action: Click Continue to Review and Confirm
Expected Result: Automatic ASW calculation runs
Calculation results and errors are shown
User cannot continue and remains on application form

Rule: Axle unit groups that fail bridge formula are indicated on the ASW table
Test Case: TC-09 - Failed axle group 3 is indicated
Preconditions: ASW calculation data is prepared so axle group 3 fails BF.
Expected Result: Calculation returns a BF failure for axle group 3
Axle unit rows 3 and 4 are indicated as failed

Rule: Axle unit groups that fail bridge formula are indicated on the ASW table
Test Case: TC-10 - Failed axle groups 3 and 4 are indicated
Preconditions: ASW calculation data is prepared so axle groups 3 and 4 fail BF.
Expected Result: Calculation returns BF failures for axle groups 3 and 4
Axle unit rows 3 and 4 and 5 are indicated as failed

Rule: Users stay on the application form when calculation on continue has errors
Test Case: TC-11 - Continue with errors keeps user on form
Preconditions: User is logged in with complete ASW data that produces calculation errors.
Action: Click Continue to Review and Confirm
Expected Result: Calculation runs and returns errors
Calculation results are shown
User cannot continue and remains on the application form

Rule: Users stay on the application form when calculation on continue has errors
Test Case: TC-12 - Continue with no errors allows navigation
Preconditions: User is logged in with complete ASW data and no calculation errors.
Action: Click Continue to Review and Confirm
Expected Result: Calculation runs successfully
Calculation results are shown
User is directed to the Review and Confirm Details page

Rule: Users are shown a warning message when they attempt to reset the ASW table
Test Case: TC-13 - Warning appears on reset
Preconditions: User has entered ASW data.
Expected Result: Reset confirmation modal appears
Warning text is shown: Reset Axle Spacing and Weights... including any additional Axle Units you may have added.
User can choose Cancel or Confirm

Rule: Users are shown a warning message when they attempt to reset the ASW table
Test Case: TC-14 - Cancel reset keeps data
Preconditions: User has entered ASW data and reset warning is displayed.
Expected Result: Reset is aborted
All previously entered ASW data remains

Rule: Users are shown a warning message when they attempt to reset the ASW table
Test Case: TC-15 - Confirm reset clears data and extras
Preconditions: User has entered ASW data, has calculation results, and has added additional axle units.
Expected Result: Reset is executed
Data is removed and table returns to default values

Action: Review calculation area and axle unit rows
Expected Result: Calculation results are removed and additional axle units are removed

Rule: Resetting the ASW table restore default field values in the table
Test Case: TC-16 - Tire sizes reset to default
Preconditions: User has entered non-default tire sizes for axle units.
Action: Click Reset ASW table and confirm
Expected Result: Reset is executed
Tire size for all axle units is reset to 279.4 mm

Rule: Resetting the ASW table restore default field values in the table
Test Case: TC-17 - Number of axles resets to default
Preconditions: User has entered non-default No. of Axles values.
Action: Click Reset ASW table and confirm
Expected Result: Reset is executed
No. of Axles is reset to 1 for all axle units

Rule: Resetting the ASW table will remove all inputted ASW data and calculation results
Test Case: TC-18 - Reset ASW with no prior calculation
Preconditions: User has inputted ASW data and has not run calculation.
Action: Click Reset ASW table and confirm
Expected Result: Reset is executed

Action: Review ASW fields and result area
Expected Result: All inputted ASW data is removed and calculation results area is cleared

Rule: Resetting the ASW table will remove all inputted ASW data and calculation results
Test Case: TC-19 - Reset ASW with prior calculation
Preconditions: User has inputted ASW data and completed a calculation.
Action: Click Reset ASW table and confirm
Expected Result: Reset is executed

Action: Review ASW fields and result area
Expected Result: All inputted ASW data is removed and calculation results are removed

Rule: Resetting the AWS table will remove the additional axle units added by the user
Test Case: TC-20 - Reset removes additional axle units
Preconditions: User has added additional axle units and entered ASW data.
Action: Click Reset ASW table and confirm
Expected Result: Reset is executed
All additional axle units are removed
ASW table shows only default power unit axle unit(s)

Rule: Only numbers can be inputted into the ASW table input fields
Test Case: TC-21 - Non-numeric text rejected
Preconditions: User is logged in and ASW input fields are editable.
Action: Enter ten in No. of Axles for axle unit 1
Expected Result: Input is not accepted as a numeric value
No. of Axles for axle unit 1 is empty

Rule: Interaxle spacing and Axle Spread have two decimal precision
Test Case: TC-22 - Axle spread whole number formats to two decimals
Preconditions: User is logged in and axle spread field for axle unit 2 is editable.
Action: Enter 10 in axle spread for axle unit 2
Expected Result: Value is accepted
Axle spread for axle unit 2 displays 10.00

Rule: Interaxle spacing and Axle Spread have two decimal precision
Test Case: TC-23 - Axle spread single decimal formats to two decimals
Preconditions: User is logged in and axle spread field for axle unit 2 is editable.
Action: Enter 5.5 in axle spread for axle unit 2
Expected Result: Value is accepted
Axle spread for axle unit 2 displays 5.50

Rule: No. of Axles No. of Wheels and Axle Unit Weight are whole numbers
Test Case: TC-24 - Decimal rounds up for axle unit weight
Preconditions: User is logged in and axle unit weight field is editable.
Action: Enter 5000.5 in axle unit weight for axle unit 1
Expected Result: Value is accepted for rounding
Axle unit weight for axle unit 1 displays 5001

Rule: No. of Axles No. of Wheels and Axle Unit Weight are whole numbers
Test Case: TC-25 - Decimal rounds down for axle unit weight
Preconditions: User is logged in and axle unit weight field is editable.
Action: Enter 5000.4 in axle unit weight for axle unit 1
Expected Result: Value is accepted for rounding
Axle unit weight for axle unit 1 displays 5000

Rule: 0 -x and empty are invalid input
Test Case: TC-26 - Zero input is invalid
Preconditions: User is logged in and ASW fields are editable.
Action: Enter 0 in No. of Axles for axle unit 2
Expected Result: Input is present but invalid for calculation
Message is shown: All fields in Axle Spacing and Weights are required to calculate results.
No. of Axles field for axle unit 2 is indicated

Rule: 0 -x and empty are invalid input
Test Case: TC-27 - All empty input is invalid
Preconditions: User is logged in and no ASW fields are filled.
Expected Result: All mandatory ASW fields remain empty
Message is shown: All fields in Axle Spacing and Weights are required to calculate results.
All empty mandatory fields are indicated

Rule: Calculate bridge calculation wheelbase (BCW) for each axle group
Test Case: TC-28 - Calculate BCW for axle group 1
Preconditions: ASW has 2 axle units. Axle unit 1 has 1 axle, interaxle spacing 4.90 m. Axle unit 2 axle spread is 1.50 m.
Action: Enter the specified ASW values for axle units 1 and 2
Expected Result: Inputs are accepted
Calculation is executed
BCW for axle group 1 is 640 cm

Rule: Calculate bridge formula (BF) for each axle group as 30 x (BCW) (cm) + 18000 kg = maximum weight allowed by permit
Test Case: TC-29 - Calculate BF for one axle group
Preconditions: Axle unit 1: axles 1, spread blank, spacing 4.90, weight 4200. Axle unit 2: axles 2, spread 1.50, spacing N/A, weight 15000.
Action: Enter the specified ASW values for axle units 1 and 2
Expected Result: Inputs are accepted
Calculation is executed
BF result is 37200

Rule: Axle groups fail BF when the sum of axle unit weights for an axle group is greater than the BF result
Test Case: TC-30 - Fail axle group 3 calculation
Preconditions: Axle unit 3: axles 2 spread 1.00 spacing 3.00 weight 15600. Axle unit 4: axles 2 spread 1.10 spacing N/A weight 17800.
Action: Enter the specified ASW values for axle units 3 and 4
Expected Result: Inputs are accepted
Calculation is executed

Action: Review BF and group weight values
Expected Result: BF result is 33300 and axle group weight is 33400
Error is shown: Bridge calculation failed for Axle Group (Axle Unit 3 - Axle Unit 4)...

Rule: Axle unit groups that fail bridge formula are indicated on the ASW
Test Case: TC-31 - ASW highlights failed axle group 3
Preconditions: Calculation setup causes axle group 3 to fail BF.
Expected Result: Calculation returns failure for axle group 3
Axle unit rows 3 and 4 are indicated

Rule: Axle unit groups that fail bridge formula are indicated on the ASW
Test Case: TC-32 - ASW highlights failed axle groups 3 and 4
Preconditions: Calculation setup causes axle groups 3 and 4 to fail BF.
Expected Result: Calculation returns failures for axle groups 3 and 4
Axle unit rows 3 and 4 and 5 are indicated

Rule: All axle groups must pass BF
Test Case: TC-33 - Failed axle group shows error
Preconditions: Calculation setup causes at least one axle group to fail BF.
Expected Result: Calculation is executed
Error is shown similar to: Bridge calculation failed for Axle Group (Axle Unit X - Axle Unit Y)...

Rule: All axle groups must pass BF
Test Case: TC-34 - All axle groups pass shows no success message
Preconditions: Calculation setup causes all axle groups to pass BF.
Expected Result: Calculation is executed
No success message is shown
